name: My Bubbe's Blintzas
recipeInstructions:
Mix the dry ingredients (salt and flour) together.
In a separate bowl, beat the eggs well. Gradually add water to the beaten eggs, stirring constantly until the batter is smooth and thin.
Heat a small frying pan over medium heat. If using Teflon, it makes life easier. Smear the pan with a very thin layer of butter.
Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the hot pan, tilting the pan to ensure even coverage. Cook until the edges begin to blister and the surface is shiny.
Carefully turn the blintza onto a plate or tablecloth (paper towels can also be used).
Mix the filling ingredients: cottage cheese, egg yolk, sugar, and salt. Beat well until combined.
Spread about 1 tablespoon of the filling off-center on each blintza.
Fold the bottom up about one-third, then fold in the sides. Finally, fold the top down to enclose the filling.
Repeat with the remaining batter, creating about 10-12 blintzas.
Store any leftover blintzas in the refrigerator or freeze for later use (they freeze well). To serve, fry each blintza in a small amount of butter until golden brown. Serve with sour cream.
